title: Notification System
description: Design a multi-channel notification system that delivers push, email, and SMS notifications at scale with priority queuing and delivery guarantees.
requirements: Send notifications via Push (APNs/FCM), Email (SES), and SMS (Twilio)
Support priority levels: critical (SLA 5s), high (30s), normal (5min)
Users can configure channel preferences and opt-out per category
Track delivery status: sent, delivered, failed, read
nonFunctionalReqs: Critical alerts delivered within 5 seconds P99
Retry with exponential backoff on delivery failure (max 3 attempts)

components.label: API Service
components.javaNote: REST endpoint POST /notifications. Validates payload, looks up user preferences, publishes NotificationEvent to Kafka with priority routing key. Returns 202 Accepted immediately.

components.label: Notification Svc
components.javaNote: Kafka consumer. Reads user preferences from DB. Fans out to per-channel topics: push-notifications, email-notifications, sms-notifications. Attaches idempotency key (SHA256 of userId+templateId+timestamp window).

components.label:
Priority Queue
(Kafka)
components.javaNote: Three Kafka topics by priority: notifications-critical (1 partition, replication=3), notifications-high, notifications-normal. Critical topic has dedicated consumer group with min.insync.replicas=2 for durability.

components.label:
Push Worker
(APNs/FCM)
components.javaNote: Connects to Apple APNs (HTTP/2) and Google FCM. Batches up to 500 tokens per FCM multicast request. On delivery failure (invalid token): remove from DB. On FCM 429: exponential backoff with jitter.

components.label:
Email Worker
(SES)
components.javaNote: AWS SES with SendBulkTemplatedEmail API. Handles bounces/complaints via SNS → SQS → worker that marks emails as invalid. DKIM/SPF/DMARC configured for deliverability.

components.label:
SMS Worker
(Twilio)
components.javaNote: Twilio Messaging API. Country-based sender ID routing. Most expensive channel — only used for critical/high priority or explicit user preference. Status callbacks update delivery status in DB.

components.label:
User Preferences
DB
components.javaNote: Postgres table: (user_id, channel, category, enabled, updated_at). Cached in Redis (5 min TTL). Schema allows per-category opt-out: user may want push for security alerts but not marketing.

componentBreakdown.heading: Fan-out with Idempotency Key
componentBreakdown.detail: The notification service fans out one event to multiple channel workers. Each delivery attempt includes an idempotency key to prevent duplicate sends on retries.

componentBreakdown.heading: FCM Batch Push with Retry
componentBreakdown.detail: FCM supports up to 500 registration tokens per multicast call. Exponential backoff with jitter on rate limits. Invalid tokens are pruned immediately to keep the token DB clean.
